title: Multi-Domain Knowledge Retrieval API with SQLite FTS5 Pattern
summary: A FastAPI-based multi-domain knowledge retrieval service pattern that unifies SQLite FTS5 full-text search across multiple knowledge domains (UI design, agent workflow, automation, backend engineering, UI assets). Core pattern: each domain has its own SQLite DB with FTS5 index, chunk result models, ranked merge logic, and a unified /brief endpoint that derives queries from natural language task descriptions using rule-based keyword matching. The architecture cleanly separates domain-specific models, search strategies, and merge/ranking logic while sharing a common request logging middleware and health check endpoint.
why_reusable: Any project that needs to serve multiple knowledge domains through a single API can reuse this architecture. The pattern of domain-per-DB with FTS5, shared ranking infrastructure, and rule-based query derivation generalizes to documentation search, internal knowledge bases, RAG backends, and AI agent tool APIs.
adapter_target: Generic knowledge retrieval service — extract the core infrastructure (connect_db, search FTS/LIKE/terms, ranked merge, query derivation) into a reusable library or service template.
implementation_notes: Each domain gets its own SQLite DB path, connect function, and Pydantic chunk model
FTS5 with content table JOIN is the primary search method; LIKE and multi-term LIKE are fallbacks
Ranked merge: per-query floor guarantees at least N results from each derived query before best-score fill
Query derivation: tuple[triggers] → query string rules with defaults when no triggers match
API request logging: structured JSONL logging to file with error tolerance (OSError pass)
Health endpoint reports DB existence, reference counts, chunk counts per domain
POST /brief accepts typed request body; GET /brief uses query params — both share build_brief_response()
tests_required: Unit test: query derivation from task text with various trigger combinations
Unit test: ranked merge preserves per-query floor and diversity
Unit test: chunk score calculation with different section/evidence/priority weights
Integration test: FTS5 search returns expected chunks for known queries
Integration test: /health reports correct counts when DBs exist and when they don't
Integration test: /brief returns balanced results across domains
risks: SQLite FTS5 has limited CJK (Chinese/Japanese/Korean) tokenization — may need ICU tokenizer or external segmentation
Multiple LIKE searches on large DBs can be slow; candidate limit must be capped
Query derivation rules are hand-maintained and may miss new task types
Domain DB paths are hardcoded at module level; multi-tenant deployments need path injection
do_not_do: Do not use FTS5 without a LIKE fallback — FTS5 can fail on special characters
Do not run searches without candidate limits — unbounded LIKE on large DBs will timeout
Do not hardcode ROOT path resolution; use config or env vars for production deployment
Do not skip the per-query floor in ranked merge — it prevents one query from dominating results
packaging_impact: FastAPI + uvicorn for deployment; SQLite is embedded, no external DB server needed
notes: Extracted from DataBase backend_api/app/main.py (1778 lines). The reusable pattern is the architecture — domain-per-DB, shared ranking, query derivation — not the specific domains or queries.
